sql: with columns `date`, `actual_sales`, and `predicted_sales`, where `predicted_sales` is calculated using a simple linear regression model, here's how you can achieve this in SQLite:

WITH CustomerOrders AS (
    SELECT 
        customer_id,
        MAX(order_date) AS last_purchase_date,
        COUNT(order_id) AS total_orders,
        SUM(sales_amount) AS total_spend
    FROM 
        orders
    WHERE 
        order_status = 'delivered'
    GROUP BY 
        customer_id
),
RFMScores AS (
    SELECT 
        customer_id,
        RANK() OVER (ORDER BY last_purchase_date DESC) AS recency_rank,
        RANK() OVER (ORDER BY total_orders DESC) AS frequency_rank,
        RANK() OVER (ORDER BY total_spend DESC) AS monetary_rank
    FROM 
        CustomerOrders
)
SELECT 
    r.customer_id,
    CASE 
        WHEN r.recency_rank <= 1 THEN 'Low Recency'
        WHEN r.recency_rank BETWEEN 2 AND 3 THEN 'Medium Recency'
        ELSE 'High Recency'
    END AS recency_segment,
    CASE 
        WHEN r.frequency_rank <= 1 THEN 'Low Frequency'
        WHEN r.frequency_rank BETWEEN 2 AND 3 THEN 'Medium Frequency'
        ELSE 'High Frequency'
    END AS frequency_segment,
    CASE 
        WHEN r.monetary_rank <= 1 THEN 'Low Monetary'
        WHEN r.monetary_rank BETWEEN 2 AND 3 THEN 'Medium Monetary'
        ELSE 'High Monetary'
    END AS monetary_segment,
    COALESCE(c.total_spend / c.total_orders, 0) AS avg_sales_per_order
FROM 
    RFMScores r
LEFT JOIN 
    CustomerOrders c ON r.customer_id = c.customer_id

SELECT 
    c.customer_id,
    COUNT(o.order_id) AS number_of_orders,
    AVG(p.amount) AS average_payment_per_order,
    CASE 
        WHEN MAX(o.purchase_date) - MIN(o.purchase_date) < INTERVAL '7 days' THEN 1.0
        ELSE (MAX(o.purchase_date) - MIN(o.purchase_date)) / INTERVAL '7 days'
    END AS customer_lifespan_in_weeks
FROM 
    customers c
JOIN 
    orders o ON c.customer_id = o.customer_id
JOIN 
    payments p ON o.order_id = p.order_id
GROUP BY 
    c.customer_id
ORDER BY 
    average_payment_per_order DESC
LIMIT 3

SELECT AVG(
    ROUND(CAST(ABS(JULIANDAY(T2.final_game_date) - JULIANDAY(T2.debut_game_date)) AS REAL) / 365, 2)
) AS average_career_span
FROM players AS T1
JOIN games AS T2 ON T1.player_id = T2.player_id

SELECT given_name, surname, MAX(games_played) AS max_games_played, 
       MAX(runs) AS max_runs, MAX(hits) AS max_hits, 
       MAX(home_runs) AS max_home_runs
FROM baseball_players

SELECT MAX(distance_km) AS longest_route_distance
FROM Routes
WHERE departure_city = 'Abakan' OR destination_city = 'Abakan'

WITH DistanceRanges AS (
    SELECT 
        city1,
        city2,
        AVG(distance) AS avg_distance
    FROM 
        Routes
    GROUP BY 
        city1, city2
),
BinnedDistances AS (
    SELECT 
        city1,
        city2,
        CASE 
            WHEN avg_distance < 1000 THEN '0-999'
            WHEN avg_distance BETWEEN 1000 AND 1999 THEN '1000-1999'
            WHEN avg_distance BETWEEN 2000 AND 2999 THEN '2000-2999'
            WHEN avg_distance BETWEEN 3000 AND 3999 THEN '3000-3999'
            WHEN avg_distance BETWEEN 4000 AND 4999 THEN '4000-4999'
            WHEN avg_distance BETWEEN 5000 AND 5999 THEN '5000-5999'
            ELSE '6000+'
        END AS distance_range
    FROM 
        DistanceRanges
)
SELECT 
    distance_range,
    COUNT(DISTINCT city1, city2) AS num_pairs
FROM 
    BinnedDistances
GROUP BY 
    distance_range
ORDER BY 
    num_pairs ASC
LIMIT 1

SELECT 
    SUM(CASE WHEN p.party_safety_equipment LIKE '%helmet%' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS helmeted_collisions,
    SUM(CASE WHEN p.party_safety_equipment NOT LIKE '%helmet%' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS unhelmeted_collisions,
    COUNT(*) AS total_collisions,
    SUM(CASE WHEN c.fatalities > 0 AND p.party_type = 'motorcyclist' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS motorcyclist_fatalities
FROM 
    collisions c
JOIN 
    parties p ON c.id = p.collision_id

WITH yearly_causes AS (
    SELECT 
        YEAR(date) AS year,
        cause,
        COUNT(*) AS count
    FROM 
        California_Traffic_Collision
    GROUP BY 
        YEAR(date), cause
),
ranked_causes AS (
    SELECT 
        year,
        cause,
        count,
        RANK() OVER (PARTITION BY year ORDER BY count DESC) AS rank
    FROM 
        yearly_causes
)
SELECT 
    year1.year AS year_with_change,
    year2.cause AS first_most_common_cause,
    year3.cause AS second_most_common_cause
FROM 
    ranked_causes year1
JOIN 
    ranked_causes year2 ON year1.year = year2.year AND year2.rank = 1
JOIN 
    ranked_causes year3 ON year1.year = year3.year AND year3.rank = 2
WHERE 
    NOT EXISTS (
        SELECT 1
        FROM ranked_causes year4
        WHERE year4.year <> year1.year
          AND year4.rank <= 2
          AND (year4.cause = year2.cause OR year4.cause = year3.cause)
    )

WITH CollisionData AS (
    SELECT 
        YEAR,
        PRIMARY_COLLISION_FACTOR_VIOLATION_CATEGORY,
        COUNT(*) AS IncidentCount
    FROM 
        California_Traffic_Collision
    WHERE 
        YEAR IN (2011, 2021)
    GROUP BY 
        YEAR, PRIMARY_COLLISION_FACTOR_VIOLATION_CATEGORY
),
TopCategoryIn2021 AS (
    SELECT 
        PRIMARY_COLLISION_FACTOR_VIOLATION_CATEGORY,
        SUM(IncidentCount) AS TotalIncidents2021
    FROM 
        CollisionData
    WHERE 
        YEAR = 2021
    GROUP BY 
        PRIMARY_COLLISION_FACTOR_VIOLATION_CATEGORY
    ORDER BY 
        TotalIncidents2021 DESC
    LIMIT 1
),
TotalIncidentsPerYear AS (
    SELECT 
        YEAR,
        SUM(IncidentCount) AS TotalIncidents
    FROM 
        CollisionData
    GROUP BY 
        YEAR
)
SELECT 
    (td2.TotalIncidents - td1.TotalIncidents) * 100.0 / td1.TotalIncidents AS DecreasePercentage
FROM 
    TopCategoryIn2021 tc
JOIN 
    CollisionData cd ON tc.PRIMARY_COLLISION_FACTOR_VIOLATION_CATEGORY = cd.PRIMARY_COLLISION_FACTOR_VIOLATION_CATEGORY AND cd.YEAR = 2021
JOIN 
    TotalIncidentsPerYear td1 ON td1.YEAR = 2011
JOIN 
    TotalIncidentsPerYear td2 ON td2.YEAR = 2021
